free_text: Direct and compound-nucleus neutrons  from  14-18 MeV
protons on Be-9, N-14, Al-27, Fe-56, In-115, Ta-181,
and Pb-208 and from 33-MeV bremsstrahlung on  Al-27,
Flight path = 55 m.
5-in. diam by 5-in. long  NE213  scintillator
was used.
Comparison of the photoneutron  spectra  with
Lecouteur's evaporation analysis was made.
Angular and  energy  distributions  of  neutrons from
(p,xn) reaction were obtained.
The details of the experimental technique.
The details of the experimental technique.
The description  of  the  LeCouteur's  formula   for
energy spectrum calculations.
Proton-induced reactions compiled.
No information.
Data were compiled in SCJAD of Saratov
State University.
Corrected  by  SD and VV:  Updated to new
date formats,  lowercase,  PART-DET deleted, REL-REF,
STATUS moved to  SUBENTs  002 - 009.
The spectra   was   divided   by   (sig(capture)   x
The  0.15  cm-thick bismuth converter was used
as a bremsstrahlung target.

The  thick  target  was  irradiated  by 33-MeV
electrons.  The source of nearly  all  the  neutrons
produced in the target is (gamma,n) reactions due to
bremsstrahlung  radiation  but  not  disintegrations
produced by electrons.
The 0.5  cm-thick  sample  of  lead  (85%   enriched
radiogenic Pb-206) was used.
